---
title: "`r params$report_title`"
author: "`r params$report_author`"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: false
    code_folding: hide
params:
  # Master Data Path
  data_path: !r here::here("1_data", "master_micro_survey.csv")
  
  # Variables
  outcome_var: "endline_score"
  group_var: "treatment_group"
  subgroup_var: "income_tier" # The demographic variable to split the data by
  
  # Policy-Facing Labels
  metric_name: "Impact on Endline Score (Treatment vs. Control)"

  # --- Report Text (all editable; leave "" to use the smart default) ---
  report_title: "Subgroup Impacts: Coefficient Plot"
  report_author: "Ibraheem Saqib Ellahi"
  chart_title: ""
  chart_subtitle: ""
  x_label: ""
  y_label: ""
  source_note: "Generated via CERP Analytics Template"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  dev.args = list(bg = "#FAF9F6"),
  fig.path = paste0(here::here("4_output", "figures"), "/"),
  fig.width = 9,
  fig.height = 5, 
  fig.retina = 3
)
```

```{r cerp-style, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat("<style>", readr::read_file(here::here("2_R", "cerp_style.css")), "</style>")
```

```{r load-and-prep}
library(tidyverse)

source(here::here("2_R", "2.5_helpers.R"), local = TRUE)

# Load data (headers/values whitespace-scrubbed) and validate YAML params
raw_data <- cerp_load(params$data_path)
cerp_validate(raw_data, params[grepl("_var$", names(params))])

# ==============================================================================
# DATA SCRUBBING & PROCESSING BLOCK
# ==============================================================================
plot_data <- raw_data %>%
  # 1. Safely drop NAs only for the specific variables used in this plot
  drop_na(!!sym(params$outcome_var), !!sym(params$group_var), !!sym(params$subgroup_var)) %>%
  
  # 2. Scrub and map messy text to strict internal computational labels
  mutate(
    Clean_Group = str_to_title(str_trim(as.character(!!sym(params$group_var)))),
    Internal_Group = case_when(
      str_detect(Clean_Group, "(?i)Treat|Intervention|1|Yes|Received") ~ "TREAT",
      TRUE ~ "CTRL"
    ),
    # Clean up the subgroup categories (e.g., standardizing "low", "Low ", "LOW")
    Subgroup = str_to_title(str_trim(as.character(!!sym(params$subgroup_var))))
  ) %>%
  
  # 3. Calculate Mean, Variance, and Count per Subgroup & Treatment Arm
  group_by(Subgroup, Internal_Group) %>%
  summarize(
    Mean = mean(!!sym(params$outcome_var), na.rm = TRUE),
    Var = var(!!sym(params$outcome_var), na.rm = TRUE),
    N = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  
  # 4. Reshape data so Treatment and Control are side-by-side for each Subgroup
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = Internal_Group,
    values_from = c(Mean, Var, N)
  ) %>%
  
  # 5. Calculate the Average Treatment Effect (ATE) and 95% Confidence Intervals
  mutate(
    ATE = Mean_TREAT - Mean_CTRL,
    # Standard Error of the difference between two means
    SE = sqrt((Var_TREAT / N_TREAT) + (Var_CTRL / N_CTRL)),
    Lower_CI = ATE - (1.96 * SE),
    Upper_CI = ATE + (1.96 * SE),
    
    # Check if the effect is statistically significant (CI doesn't cross zero)
    Is_Sig = ifelse(Lower_CI > 0 | Upper_CI < 0, "Significant", "Not Significant"),
    
    # Sort the subgroups from highest impact to lowest for a clean visual hierarchy
    Subgroup = fct_reorder(Subgroup, ATE)
  )
```


```{r plot-coefficient}
source(here::here("2_R", "2.0_setup_theme.R"), local = TRUE)

ggplot(plot_data, aes(y = Subgroup, x = ATE)) +
  
  # The Null Effect Line: Anchors the entire chart at zero difference
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = cerp_cols[["text"]], linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) +
  
  # Draw the 95% Confidence Intervals
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = Lower_CI, xmax = Upper_CI, color = Is_Sig), 
                 height = 0, linewidth = 2) +
  
  # Draw the Point Estimate (Average Treatment Effect)
  geom_point(aes(color = Is_Sig), size = 6) +
  
  # Direct labels showing the exact numerical impact
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%+0.1f", ATE)), 
            vjust = -1.5, fontface = "bold", size = 4) +
  
  # Color-code based on significance (Grey if it touches zero, Primary color if significant)
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "Significant" = unname(cerp_cols["primary"]),
    "Not Significant" = unname(cerp_cols["neutral"])
  )) +
  
  theme_cerp() +
  
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank()
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = if (nzchar(params$chart_title)) params$chart_title else (paste("Subgroup Impact:", params$metric_name)),
    subtitle = if (nzchar(params$chart_subtitle)) params$chart_subtitle else ("Comparing average treatment effects across demographics.\nGrey lines cross zero, indicating no statistically significant impact for that group."),
    x = if (nzchar(params$x_label)) params$x_label else ("Difference (Treatment - Control)"),
    y = if (nzchar(params$y_label)) params$y_label else (NULL),
    caption = params$source_note
  )
```
